description_long: This cabin features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The upper facade is clad in red tiles, while the lower portion is constructed from dark, rough-hewn stone blocks, suggesting durability and traditional craftsmanship. The front side has a centered wooden door accessed by a small set of stairs, flanked by two small, square windows. The side walls are simpler, each with a single small window and no additional ornamentation. The foundation appears to be made of a coarser stone, providing a sturdy base. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for residential use in a rural or semi-urban setting.
description: A rustic stone and tile cabin with a steep gable roof, red-tiled upper facade, and small windows flanking a central door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central wooden door, two small square windows, red-tiled upper facade, and stone lower walls with a small staircase.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the steep gable roof, stone wall, and a single small window; no door or porch visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with red-tiled upper facade, stone lower wall, and two small windows; no door or stairs present.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with a single small window and uninterrupted stone wall beneath the roof.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, covered in reddish-brown shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: four small square windows, two on the front facade flanking the door and one on each side wall, symmetrically placed
attribute_door_placement: single wooden door centered on the front facade, accessed by stairs
